task_completion.description: Fetch or download the PDB structure 2ZTA.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL interface is open with the structure '2ZTA' loaded, visible in the right-hand object menu or the main viewer.

task_completion.description: Show both chains as cartoon with distinct colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The two parallel helices (Chain A and Chain B) are displayed in the 'cartoon' representation and are colored differently from each other.

task_completion.description: Display core hydrophobic residues (leucines/valines) as sticks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Specific side chains between the two helices (the hydrophobic core, specifically Leucines 5, 12, 19, 26) are shown in 'stick' representation, protruding inward to form the zipper.
A dashed measurement line is visible between the alpha-carbons of two residues on opposite chains, with a numerical distance value (in Angstroms) displayed on the screen.

task_completion.description: Save the figure as a PNG to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Command line in PyMOL or terminal showing the 'png' command saving to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/gcn4_coiled_coil.png', or a file browser showing the created image file.

task_completion.description: Write a structural analysis report to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the creation of '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/gcn4_coiled_coil_report.txt' containing the PDB ID (2ZTA), chain identifiers, a list of core residues (e.g., Leucines 5, 12, 19, 26), and a measured distance.

integrity.description: The agent used PyMOL to visualize and measure the structure rather than just writing a script without verifying the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL GUI open with the structure actively being manipulated, colored, and measured.

integrity.description: The distance measurement in the report matches the actual measurement taken in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical value written in the text report corresponds to the measurement label visible in the PyMOL viewer.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply hallucinate the report contents from prior knowledge.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the sequence viewer or selects residues in PyMOL to identify the core Leucines before writing them into the report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct PDB ID is 2ZTA. The key Leucine residues at the 'd' positions of the heptad repeat driving dimerization are residues 5, 12, 19, and 26. The agent must identify these and show them as sticks. The agent must perform at least one C-alpha to C-alpha distance measurement between the two chains; true distances for the innermost core residues are ~5.4 to 6.5 Angstroms, while outer positions can range up to 14.5 Angstroms.
